meta.title: sens, sent

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 2 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: sens, sent
root.mentor_note: sens and sent both mean feel, one root behind everything from senses to sentiment.

attempt.direction_prompt: Numb to what’s happening, or acutely feeling it?

context_sentences: The instrument was sensitive enough to register a footstep two rooms away.
He had grown more sensitive to criticism since the review, flinching at even mild feedback.
Public sentiment had shifted sharply against the plan within a single week of coverage.
Beneath the formal wording, the letter carried a sentiment of real, unmistakable gratitude.
The debate over whether the animal was truly sentient shaped the entire welfare policy.
The film’s central question, never fully answered, is whether the machine has become sentient.

construct_options.text: Able to perceive or feel things; conscious
Completely unable to feel or perceive anything

context_sentences: The therapy room was designed for sensory calm, with soft light and almost no background noise.
After the injury, sensory feedback in his left hand took the better part of a year to return.

construct_options.text: Relating to physical sensation or the senses
Relating to an opinion shaped strongly by emotion
